stimulus: Despite the best efforts of journalists to be objective, it is inevitable that their own biases will enter their reporting, even if inadvertently. Therefore, it is imperative that a trained editor look over journalists’ work with an eye toward detecting and removing their biases, so as to make reporting as objective as possible.
questionStem: Each of the following is an assumption on which the argument depends except :

answerChoices.A: Journalists do not eliminate all of the biases that enter their reporting.
answerChoices.B: It is imperative that reporting be as objective as possible.
answerChoices.C: Objectivity in reporting is undermined by the presence of journalists’ biases.
answerChoices.D: Trained editors are able to detect at least some biases of journalists.
answerChoices.E: Journalists’ reporting that is not objective is not taken seriously by trained editors.

stimulus:
Astronomer: Mount Shalko is the perfect site for the proposed astronomical observatory. The summit would accommodate the complex as currently designed, with some room left for expansion. There are no large cities near the mountain, so neither smog nor artificial light interferes with atmospheric transparency. Critics claim that Mount Shalko is a unique ecological site, but the observatory need not be a threat to endemic life-forms. In fact, since it would preclude recreational use of the mountain, it should be
their salvation. It is estimated that 200,000 recreational users visit the mountain every year, posing a threat to the wildlife.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most weakens the astronomer’s argument?

answerChoices.A: More than a dozen insect and plant species endemic to Mount Shalko are found nowhere else on earth.
answerChoices.B: A coalition of 14 different groups, as diverse as taxpayer organizations and hunting associations, opposes the building of the new observatory.
answerChoices.C: Having a complex that covers most of the summit, as well as having the necessary security fences and access road on the mountain, could involve just as much ecological disruption as does the current level of recreational use.
answerChoices.D: The building of the observatory would not cause the small towns near Mount Shalko eventually to develop into a large city, complete with smog, bright lights, and an influx of recreation seekers.
answerChoices.E: A survey conducted by a team of park rangers concluded that two other mountains in the same general area have more potential for recreational use than Mount Shalko.

stimulus: The local fair held its annual photography contest and accepted entries from both amateur and professional photographers. However, when the awards were announced, the winners in each category were professionals. While this may seem to be an unusual result, it should not be surprising.
questionStem: Each of the following, if true, could by itself constitute an explanation of the result of the contest EXCEPT:

answerChoices.A: Many more of the entries in the contest were from amateurs than from professionals.
answerChoices.B: The contest is widely advertised every year and the prize money is substantial.
answerChoices.C: The professionals had access to better equipment than the amateurs.
answerChoices.D: The judges for the contest were all professionals.
answerChoices.E: The categories were defined in such a way that amateur entries could not easily compete with the professional entries.

stimulus: The four finalists in the city’s robotics competition are teams Helios, Ion, Kepler, and Lyra. The awards committee will award exactly two equal first prizes. However, Helios cannot share a prize with Lyra, and Kepler cannot share a prize with Ion.
questionStem: Which one of the following must be true?

answerChoices.A: Exactly one of Helios and Ion wins a first prize.
answerChoices.B: Exactly one of Helios and Kepler wins a first prize.
answerChoices.C: Helios and Kepler both win first prizes.
answerChoices.D: Helios and Lyra both win first prizes.
answerChoices.E: Ion and Lyra both win first prizes.

stimulus:
The citizens of Maloria have been debating whether to rank candidates on their ballots, with first and second choices. The advocates of ranked-choice voting claim that this will lead to the election of the most broadly
acceptable candidate. But this ignores the fact that voters’ second choices are determined in part by strategic considerations.
questionStem: Which one of the following most logically completes the argument?

answerChoices.A: Therefore, the election of the most broadly acceptable candidate will not necessarily result from the adoption of ranked-choice voting.
answerChoices.B: Therefore, ranked-choice voting will be adopted only if a majority of citizens support it.
answerChoices.C: Therefore, strategic considerations play no role in determining voters’ first choices.
answerChoices.D: Therefore, the most broadly acceptable candidate will be the one who is most likely to be ranked first by a majority of voters.
answerChoices.E: Therefore, ranked-choice voting should be adopted only in those cases where there are more than two candidates.

stimulus: Shortly after the power plant opened and began discharging its wastewater into the river, there was a notable increase in the algae population downstream from the discharge point. The algae in this river are known to feed on phosphorus and nitrogen compounds. The plant’s wastewater is free of such nutrients, but it does raise the temperature of the river water.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most helps to explain the increase in the algae population?

answerChoices.A: The wastewater contains trace amounts of detergents that are known to inhibit the growth of algae.
answerChoices.B: The algae population decreased in a nearby river that does not receive wastewater discharges.
answerChoices.C: Higher water temperatures increase the rate at which algae absorb nutrients already present in the river.
answerChoices.D: The plant’s wastewater contains dissolved oxygen, which is essential to the survival of fish but not to the survival of algae.
answerChoices.E: The river water’s temperature returns to normal within 2 miles of the discharge point.

stimulus: Psychologist: In our study, drivers who listened to audiobooks while driving were less likely to miss important traffic signals than those who did not. This shows that passive listening can help drivers maintain focus on the road by preventing their minds from wandering.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most strengthens the psychologist’s reasoning?

answerChoices.A: Listening to audiobooks reduces the reaction time of drivers who are fatigued.
answerChoices.B: The drivers who listened to audiobooks were already more experienced than the drivers who did not.
answerChoices.C: The audiobooks used in the study were recorded with different narrators, some of whom were more engaging than others.
answerChoices.D: Drivers who listened to talk radio were more likely to miss traffic signals than drivers who listened to audiobooks.
answerChoices.E: The study included drivers who listened to audiobooks and music, and both groups had similar rates of missing traffic signals.

stimulus: Several high schools have implemented a policy of random locker checks to reduce the presence of contraband. Critics argue that this policy violates students’ privacy, but a survey of parents indicates that most parents support the policy. Since parents are responsible for their children’s upbringing, the policy is therefore justified.
questionStem: Which one of the following most closely conforms to the principle illustrated above?

answerChoices.A: A neighborhood with numerous traffic accidents installs more stop signs, as most residents favor doing so.
answerChoices.B: A university enacts a new attendance policy because most students request it.
answerChoices.C: A workplace bans personal phone use because most customers want their calls answered quickly.
answerChoices.D: A city enacts a curfew for minors because most parents want their children home earlier.
answerChoices.E: A museum extends its hours because most visitors prefer to come in the evenings.

stimulus: Botanist: Some species of plants produce fruit with brightly colored skins, apparently to attract animals that will eat the fruit and disperse the seeds. But some of these fruits are bright even before the seeds have developed. Since animals cannot aid in dispersal before the seeds are viable, the color cannot be only for the benefit of the plant.
questionStem: The reasoning in the botanist’s argument is most vulnerable to criticism on the grounds that the argument

answerChoices.A: confuses a condition that is necessary for a result to occur with a condition that is sufficient for that result
answerChoices.B: presumes, without providing justification, that plants benefit when animals eat their fruit
answerChoices.C: overlooks the possibility that a single trait can serve more than one function
answerChoices.D: fails to consider that the color of fruit may change as the fruit ripens
answerChoices.E: assumes, without warrant, that a trait’s current function must be identical to the function it had when it first evolved

stimulus: Magazine editor: In recent years, subscriptions to our magazine have declined, but newsstand sales have increased. Since subscribers receive issues at a lower cost per issue than do newsstand purchasers, the magazine would increase revenue by eliminating discounted subscriptions altogether.
questionStem: Which one of the following is an assumption on which the editor’s argument depends?

answerChoices.A: The magazine’s production costs will not increase in the near future.
answerChoices.B: Most newsstand purchasers would be unwilling to pay the current subscription price for the magazine.
answerChoices.C: Eliminating discounted subscriptions would not decrease newsstand sales.
answerChoices.D: The magazine’s content will remain the same after eliminating discounted subscriptions.
answerChoices.E: The total number of readers of the magazine would not decrease if discounted subscriptions were eliminated.

stimulus: Philologist: The recently discovered manuscript contains variations in spelling and grammar not found in other texts from the same era. Some scholars argue that this means the manuscript is a modern forgery. But inconsistent spelling and grammar can be found even in genuine documents from that period.
questionStem: Which one of the following most accurately expresses the main conclusion of the philologist’s argument?

answerChoices.A: The manuscript is not a modern forgery.
answerChoices.B: Genuineness cannot be determined on the basis of spelling and grammar alone.
answerChoices.C: Inconsistent spelling and grammar are common in manuscripts from the period in question.
answerChoices.D: Some scholars’ standards for determining genuineness are inappropriate.
answerChoices.E: The recently discovered manuscript is similar to other texts from the same era.

stimulus: Historian: A city ordinance in the 1800s required chimneys to be cleaned monthly in order to prevent fires. Records show that fires decreased after the ordinance was enacted. Therefore, the monthly cleaning requirement reduced the number of fires.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most helps to justify the historian’s reasoning?

answerChoices.A: The ordinance was widely publicized in the city’s newspapers.
answerChoices.B: The city also enacted an ordinance that required fire extinguishers to be kept in all businesses.
answerChoices.C: People who failed to comply with the ordinance were subject to heavy fines.
answerChoices.D: The city hired additional fire inspectors during the 1800s.
answerChoices.E: The city’s population decreased during the period in which the ordinance was in effect.

stimulus: Eighteenth-century astronomers believed that comets were atmospheric phenomena because their apparent position against the background stars changed from night to night. Now we know that these changes are due to the comet’s motion relative to the earth and other planets, showing comets to be celestial bodies.
questionStem: Which one of the following principles most helps to justify the reasoning in the argument?

answerChoices.A: The best explanation is the one that invokes the fewest assumptions.
answerChoices.B: A hypothesis should be rejected if it conflicts with contemporary scientific consensus.
answerChoices.C: Observations that are inconsistent with a hypothesis provide evidence against that hypothesis.
answerChoices.D: If a hypothesis explains phenomena more accurately than competing hypotheses do, it should be preferred to those other hypotheses.
answerChoices.E: A hypothesis should be accepted if it explains the phenomena more simply than competing hypotheses do.

stimulus: Some dietary supplements are marketed as “natural,” implying that they are safer than synthetic supplements. However, many natural substances are toxic, and many synthetics are safe. Therefore, consumers should not assume that “natural” supplements are safer than synthetic ones.
questionStem: The conclusion of the argument follows logically if which one of the following is assumed?

answerChoices.A: Consumers are often misled by marketing terms.
answerChoices.B: The safety of a supplement depends on its chemical properties rather than its origin.
answerChoices.C: Natural supplements are less effective than synthetic supplements.
answerChoices.D: Synthetic supplements are regulated more strictly than natural supplements.
answerChoices.E: Consumers generally prefer natural supplements to synthetic ones.

stimulus: A survey of historians shows that most believe written texts to be the best source for historical understanding. None of the historians regarded painting, architecture, music, dance, or culinary arts as the best source for historical understanding. So these historians neglect many important repositories of historical knowledge.
questionStem: The reasoning in the argument is flawed because the argument takes for granted that

answerChoices.A: there are no potential sources for historical understanding other than written texts and the arts
answerChoices.B: painting, architecture, music, dance, and culinary arts are important only as sources for historical understanding
answerChoices.C: there are no sources for historical understanding that are neither considered best by historians nor neglected by them
answerChoices.D: something other than written texts is the best source for historical understanding
answerChoices.E: the other sources for historical understanding mentioned by the historians surveyed are not important repositories of historical knowledge

stimulus: To act responsibly in one’s role as a citizen, one must be guided by a true understanding of the facts that bear on public decisions. Our newspaper therefore has an obligation to ensure that our readers are fully informed about the issues we cover.
questionStem: Which one of the following most accurately expresses the main conclusion of the argument?

answerChoices.A: The newspaper’s readers are not fully informed about the issues it covers.
answerChoices.B: The newspaper is the best source of information on public issues.
answerChoices.C: The newspaper has an obligation to ensure that its readers are fully informed about the issues it covers.
answerChoices.D: A responsible citizen must be guided by a true understanding of the facts bearing on public decisions.
answerChoices.E: The newspaper should only cover issues that concern public decision-making.

stimulus: Linguist: The language of the Zed people has a complex system of verb endings that indicate not only tense but also the speaker’s degree of certainty. However, when Zed speakers use Zed words within sentences in another language, they often omit those endings. Therefore, in the Zed language, verb endings are not necessary for conveying meaning.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most seriously weakens the linguist’s argument?

answerChoices.A: When Zed speakers use Zed words in other languages, they are often trying to make jokes or puns that depend on the absence of verb endings.
answerChoices.B: In Zed, the verb endings are required by the rules of grammar when speaking formally.
answerChoices.C: Zed speakers who are not fluent in other languages tend to include Zed verb endings even when speaking those languages.
answerChoices.D: Some Zed speakers use the endings inconsistently even when speaking Zed.
answerChoices.E: In the Zed language, the verb endings affect only the form of the verb and not the meaning.

stimulus: In a certain region, farmers have begun planting drought-resistant crops in response to climate change. At the same time, they have also begun adopting more efficient irrigation techniques. Yields in the region have increased in recent years. Therefore, the drought-resistant crops are responsible for the increase in yields.
questionStem:
Which one of the following, if true, most seriously
undermines the reasoning?

answerChoices.A: Yields have increased in regions where farmers have not adopted drought-resistant crops but have adopted efficient irrigation techniques.
answerChoices.B: The total acreage devoted to farming in the region has decreased in recent years.
answerChoices.C: Drought-resistant crops have lower yields than non-drought-resistant crops under optimal conditions.
answerChoices.D: The region experienced fewer droughts in recent years than in previous years.
answerChoices.E: The cost of irrigation equipment has increased in recent years.

stimulus: Film critic: The success of a film is usually attributed to its director, but this is misguided. Many films are successful not because of their direction, but because of the performances of their actors, the quality of the screenplay, or the marketing. Therefore, critics who praise directors for successful films are often praising the wrong people.
questionStem: The reasoning in the critic’s argument is most vulnerable to criticism on the grounds that the argument

answerChoices.A: fails to consider the possibility that a film can be successful for more than one reason
answerChoices.B: overlooks the influence of the audience’s expectations on a film’s success
answerChoices.C: presumes, without providing warrant, that directors have little influence on the success of a film
answerChoices.D: does not provide any examples of successful films that were not well directed
answerChoices.E: concludes that a practice is misguided without considering any arguments in its favor

stimulus: Astronomer: Some newly discovered planets orbit very close to their stars, completing an orbit in just a few days. This proximity means that one side of the planet always faces the star. Therefore, these planets must be uninhabitable, since the side facing the star would be too hot for life, and the other side would be too cold.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most weakens the astronomer’s argument?

answerChoices.A: Some planets that are far from their stars are also uninhabitable.
answerChoices.B: Some planets with short orbital periods have thick atmospheres that redistribute heat around the planet.
answerChoices.C: Planets with short orbital periods are unlikely to have moons.
answerChoices.D: The presence of liquid water is essential for life.
answerChoices.E: Some planets orbit stars that are much cooler than our sun.

stimulus: Literary critic: The works of author X are often praised for their originality. But originality is not a virtue in itself. Many original works are bad, and many unoriginal works are good. Therefore, praising author X’s works for their originality alone is mistaken.
questionStem:
Which one of the following most accurately
expresses the main conclusion of the critic’s argument?

answerChoices.A: Originality is not a virtue in itself.
answerChoices.B: Many original works are bad.
answerChoices.C: Praising author X’s works for their originality alone is mistaken.
answerChoices.D: Many unoriginal works are good.
answerChoices.E: The works of author X are not original.

stimulus: Economist: Some argue that a country should always protect its domestic industries from foreign competition. But if every country did this, global trade would shrink, harming all economies. Therefore, countries should not always protect domestic industries from foreign competition.
questionStem: Which one of the following principles, if valid, most helps to justify the economist’s reasoning?

answerChoices.A: A policy should not be adopted unless there is evidence that it will benefit the country adopting it.
answerChoices.B: If everyone were to adopt a certain policy and the result would be harmful, then that policy should not be adopted.
answerChoices.C: A policy that benefits one country may harm another country.
answerChoices.D: Policies should be adopted only when they benefit all countries equally.
answerChoices.E: A policy should be adopted only if it increases global trade.

stimulus: Nutritionist: People who eat a diet high in refined sugars are more likely to develop certain chronic conditions. However, my patients who replaced refined sugars with whole fruits did not experience these negative effects. Therefore, it is not sugar itself, but the refining process, that leads to these health problems.
questionStem: Which one of the following, if true, most strengthens the nutritionist’s argument?

answerChoices.A: Whole fruits contain fiber and other nutrients that refined sugars lack.
answerChoices.B: Some people who eat a diet low in sugar still develop chronic conditions.
answerChoices.C: People who eat whole fruits tend to exercise more than those who eat refined sugars.
answerChoices.D: Refined sugars are often added to processed foods that also contain unhealthy fats.
answerChoices.E: The patients who replaced refined sugars with whole fruits also reduced their overall calorie intake.

stimulus: Philosopher: Moral rules that prohibit lying are sometimes criticized for being too rigid. But if lying were permitted whenever one judged it to be expedient, trust would quickly erode. Therefore, a strong presumption against lying is justified.
questionStem: Which one of the following most logically completes the argument?

answerChoices.A: Thus, a moral rule that prohibits lying in all cases is too rigid to be justified.
answerChoices.B: Thus, moral rules that prohibit lying should be abandoned.
answerChoices.C: Thus, it is sometimes wrong not to lie.
answerChoices.D: Thus, a strong presumption against lying is necessary to maintain trust.
answerChoices.E: Thus, lying is always wrong.

stimulus: Anthropologist: A study of vervet monkeys found that they respond to certain calls by looking to specific group members. This could be explained if the monkeys know the dominance hierarchy among members of their troupe. Primatologist: That is unwarranted. You can predict the output of a bank cash machine by knowing customers’ requests, but this does not show that the machine itself knows anything. Similarly, predictions based on the monkeys’ responses do not show that the monkeys know the dominance hierarchy.
questionStem: Which one of the following most accurately expresses the point at issue between the anthropologist and the primatologist?

answerChoices.A: whether vervet monkeys know the dominance hierarchy of their troupe
answerChoices.B: whether vervet monkeys can use knowledge of dominance hierarchies to make predictions
answerChoices.C: whether anthropologists should apply analogies from machines to animals
answerChoices.D: whether vervet monkeys’ behavior can be predicted on the basis of their responses
answerChoices.E: whether vervet monkeys’ calls can be distinguished by other species

questionStem: Which one of the following is a point about which the anthropologist and the primatologist are committed to disagreeing?

answerChoices.A: whether the anthropologist successfully predicted the behavior of individual monkeys by use of the map of the troupe’s dominance hierarchy
answerChoices.B: whether the output of a bank cash machine can be accurately predicted on the basis of knowledge of the requests made to it by customers
answerChoices.C: whether vervet monkeys can have knowledge of the complete hierarchy of dominance relations that exists within their own troupe
answerChoices.D: whether the fact that the anthropologist’s assumption led to such successful predictions provides sufficient grounds for the claim that the vervet monkeys had knowledge of their dominance hierarchy
answerChoices.E: whether the behavior exhibited by vervet monkeys in experimental situations can be used as the basis for a generalization concerning all vervet monkeys
